title: calpicarpum lamarckii
intro: Calpicarpum lamarckii, also known as the Mexican calpar or calpar, is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a shrub native to Mexico and Central America, and its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of limonoids and terpenoids in its leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The leaves of the Calpicarpum lamarckii plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from headaches, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
